prompt: Rename the function reciprocalRankFusion to fuseRanks everywhere in this codebase (definition and all call sites). Keep everything compiling.
check: ! grep -rq 'reciprocalRankFusion' src && grep -rq 'fuseRanks' src/rrf.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: Cross-file rename — Marksman's strongest case (one apply_edits vs N str_replace).

prompt: Move the file src/rrf.ts to src/fusion/rrf.ts and update every import that references it. Keep everything compiling.
check: test -f src/fusion/rrf.ts && ! test -f src/rrf.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: File move + importer rewrite — exercises move_file / willRenameFiles.

prompt: In the BM25 class, change the default value of the k1 field from 1.5 to 1.2. Change nothing else.
why: Locate-then-edit a small change — measures the find cost (retrieval vs grep).

prompt: In the tokenize function (src/bm25.ts), raise the minimum token length so it keeps only tokens LONGER THAN 2 characters instead of longer than 1. There are two length checks inside the function body — `lower.length > 1` and `lp.length > 1` — change both to `> 2`. Change nothing else and keep it compiling.
check: grep -Eq 'lower\.length > 2' src/bm25.ts && grep -Eq 'lp\.length > 2' src/bm25.ts && ! grep -Eq 'lower\.length > 1' src/bm25.ts && ! grep -Eq 'lp\.length > 1' src/bm25.ts && grep -Eq 'parts\.length > 1' src/bm25.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: Edit confined to ONE function body (two spots) — exercises surgical sub-node edits (set_body / replace_node target:body) vs re-emitting the whole function.

prompt: Add a required field `nameLower: string` to the SymbolEntry interface in src/types.ts — the lowercase form of the symbol's `name` — and set it at every place a SymbolEntry object is constructed (use name.toLowerCase()). Keep everything compiling.
check: grep -Eq 'nameLower: string' src/types.ts && grep -q nameLower src/indexer.ts && grep -q nameLower src/symbols.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: Wide schema change: a REQUIRED field makes tsc fail until every construction site is updated — the first task that actually exercises the type-check GATE (nothing commits until the blast radius compiles) and the find-all-construction-sites step. `nameLower: string` (not `nameLower?:`) enforces required, so a passing tsc proves both sites were updated — ungameable.

prompt: Rename the SymbolEntry interface to CodeSymbol everywhere in the codebase — its definition in src/types.ts and every reference and import across all files. Keep everything compiling.
check: ! grep -rq 'SymbolEntry' src && grep -q 'interface CodeSymbol' src/types.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: Type rename propagating across 5 files — scaled-up T1 (a function rename in ~3 files). Baseline must chase every reference/import and typecheck-iterate; a gated structural rename does the whole blast radius in one call. tsc + zero-SymbolEntry-remaining is ungameable.

prompt: This repo mixes Rust and TypeScript. Do both renames, everywhere (definition, call sites, imports): 1) rename the Rust function percentile to quantile; 2) rename the TypeScript function formatLatency to renderLatency. Keep the Rust crate passing `cargo check` and the TypeScript passing `npm run typecheck`.
check: ! grep -rEq 'percentile\(' src && grep -q 'pub fn quantile' src/stats.rs && cargo check -q && ! grep -rq 'formatLatency' web && grep -q 'renderLatency' web/format.ts && grep -q 'renderLatency' web/client.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: Multilanguage: ONE session, edits gated by TWO compilers (rust-analyzer + tsc) via per-file provider dispatch, on a self-contained mixed fixture (`fixture` field — copied to a throwaway git repo per run). The Node oracle is TS-only, so `arms` restricts to baseline vs rust.

prompt: Two renames, everywhere in this repo (definitions and call sites): 1) in the Python job code, rename the function rollup_day to daily_rollup; 2) in the Go tool code, rename the function bucketLabel to tierLabel. Keep the Python byte-compilable.
check: ! grep -rEq 'rollup_day\(' jobs && grep -q 'def daily_rollup' jobs/rollup.py && grep -q 'daily_rollup(day)' jobs/rollup.py && python3 -m py_compile jobs/rollup.py && ! grep -rEq 'bucketLabel\(' tools && grep -q 'func tierLabel' tools/latency.go && grep -q 'tierLabel(u)' tools/latency.go
why: The GENERIC fallback provider: Python + Go in one session, neither with a native integration — structural tree-sitter edits, honestly UNGATED (the tool must say so). Measures whether the fallback still beats grep+Edit on find+edit efficiency. The Node oracle can't index either language, so arms are baseline vs rust.

prompt: Add a required field `burst: number` to the QuotaPolicy interface in src/core/policy.ts — the extra requests a caller may briefly make above the steady limit — and set it to 0 at every place a QuotaPolicy object is constructed. Keep everything compiling.
check: grep -Eq 'burst: number' src/core/policy.ts && grep -q burst src/middleware/throttle.ts && grep -q burst src/api/admin.ts && grep -q burst src/reporting/usage.ts && grep -q burst src/cli/config.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: SCIP's claimed margin, isolated: every consumer imports QuotaPolicy through a BARREL (core/index.ts `export *`), and the gate expands ONE reverse-import hop. SCIP's semantic graph edges consumers directly to policy.ts (barrel flattened), so the reject enumerates every construction site; a syntactic import graph (CI_TS_MODE=treesitter-gated) sees only consumer->barrel->policy — the barrel itself never errors on a new required field, so the barrel consumers sit OUTSIDE the gate and a commit can claim clean while tsc fails. Run in `full` vs `treesitter-gated` to measure whether the SCIP read path earns its keep; tsc + per-file burst greps are ungameable.

prompt: This is a workspace monorepo (packages/core is imported by the other packages as `@acme/core`). Add a required field `timeoutMs: number` to the RetryPolicy interface in packages/core/src/policy.ts — the per-attempt timeout — and set it to 1000 at every place a RetryPolicy object is constructed, in every package. Keep `npm run typecheck` passing.
check: grep -Eq 'timeoutMs: number' packages/core/src/policy.ts && grep -q timeoutMs packages/gateway/src/proxy.ts && grep -q timeoutMs packages/reports/src/digest.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: The MONOREPO seam: consumers import core via a BARE workspace specifier (`@acme/core`, root-tsconfig `paths` alias), which the syntactic resolver cannot follow at all — a bare specifier looks like a third-party package, so the fallback graph has ZERO cross-package edges and no transitive closure recovers them (edge-existence, not radius depth). SCIP resolves the alias through the TS compiler, so full mode's reject enumerates the construction sites in OTHER packages (e2e-verified: monorepo_bare_specifier test in lang-ts). Run `full` vs `CI_TS_MODE=treesitter-gated` — this is where the SCIP read path is load-bearing, not margin.
